subject: 🏃 Thursday — Easy 5 mi (first run back)
html:
Today: Easy 5 mi — your first run in 4 days. Pace target is 9:15–10:00/mi on flat ground, HR under 140. Run on a soft surface if you can — Sawyer Camp Trail or Bayfront paths. This is not a fitness run; it's a knee diagnostic.

Focus cue: Tune into the knee from step one. If you feel any tingle, awareness, or ache during the run, cut it to 3 mi and walk home. No negotiation. The plan explicitly says to drop to 3 mi or rest if the knee is still talking — that rule is live today.

Context: Week 1 of 15, Base phase, 101 days to race day. The knee went Yellow after the Cambria run Saturday and again Monday afternoon after Lower A, but was Green during Tuesday's Upper Push. Four days off from running plus two gym-only sessions is solid recovery time — the trend is positive, but the run is the real test.

Looking ahead: Tomorrow is Lower B + Upper Push, Saturday is Easy 5, Sunday is the Long 8. All of that hinges on how the knee responds today. A clean Green after this run and the rest of the week is full go. Report back with knee status after the run so I can greenlight or modify the weekend.

Go easy. Listen to the knee. Report back.
